## Data Visualization Best Practices (Deep Dive)

### Chart Selection Guide

```python
class ChartSelectionGuide:
    """
    Comprehensive guide for selecting appropriate chart types
    """
    
    CHART_TYPES = {
        # Comparisons
        'bar': {
            'purpose': 'Compare discrete categories',
            'best_for': ['Category comparison', 'Ranking', 'Data with long labels'],
            'avoid_when': ['Continuous data', 'Many categories (>20)', 'Time series'],
            'examples': ['Sales by product', 'Performance by team', 'Survey responses'],
            'variations': ['grouped', 'stacked', 'horizontal', 'diverging']
        },
        'column': {
            'purpose': 'Compare values across categories',
            'best_for': ['Time-based comparison', 'Few categories', 'Negative values'],
            'avoid_when': ['Many time periods', 'Long category names'],
            'examples': ['Monthly revenue', 'Year-over-year comparison'],
            'variations': ['grouped', 'stacked', 'waterfall', 'bullet']
        },
        'line': {
            'purpose': 'Show trends over time',
            'best_for': ['Time series data', 'Continuous data', 'Multiple series'],
            'avoid_when': ['Categorical data', 'Few data points'],
            'examples': ['Stock prices', 'Website traffic', 'Temperature trends'],
            'variations': ['multi-line', 'area', 'step', 'sparkline']
        },
        
        # Distributions
        'histogram': {
            'purpose': 'Show frequency distribution',
            'best_for': ['Data distribution', 'Identifying outliers', 'Understanding spread'],
            'avoid_when': ['Small datasets (<30)', 'Categorical data'],
            'examples': ['Age distribution', 'Order values', 'Response times'],
            'variations': ['frequency polygon', 'cumulative']
        },
        'box_plot': {
            'purpose': 'Summarize distribution statistics',
            'best_for': ['Comparing distributions', 'Identifying outliers', 'Statistical summary'],
            'avoid_when': ['Non-technical audiences', 'Small datasets'],
            'examples': ['Salary by department', 'Scores by class', 'Sales by region'],
            'variations': ['violin plot', 'bean plot']
        },
        'violin': {
            'purpose': 'Show distribution shape and density',
            'best_for': ['Detailed distribution comparison', 'Large datasets'],
            'avoid_when': ['Simple comparisons', 'Small datasets'],
            'examples': ['User engagement by segment', 'Order values by category'],
            'variations': ['split violin', 'grouped violin']
        },
        
        # Proportions
        'pie': {
            'purpose': 'Show part-to-whole relationships',
            'best_for': ['Few categories (3-5)', 'Simple proportions', 'Percentages'],
            'avoid_when': ['Many categories', 'Similar sizes', 'Precise comparison needed'],
            'examples': ['Market share', 'Budget allocation'],
            'variations': ['donut', 'sunburst', 'treemap']
        },
        'treemap': {
            'purpose': 'Show hierarchical proportions',
            'best_for': ['Hierarchical data', 'Many categories', 'Space-efficient'],
            'avoid_when': ['Precise comparison', 'No hierarchy'],
            'examples': ['Product categories', 'File system usage'],
            'variations': ['nested', 'icicle']
        },
        'stacked_bar': {
            'purpose': 'Show composition and totals',
            'best_for': ['Part-to-whole', 'Category comparison', 'Trends over time'],
            'avoid_when': ['Many segments', 'Segments similar size'],
            'examples': ['Revenue by channel', 'Traffic sources'],
            'variations': ['100% stacked', 'grouped stacked']
        },
        
        # Relationships
        'scatter': {
            'purpose': 'Show correlation between variables',
            'best_for': ['Correlation analysis', 'Outlier detection', 'Large datasets'],
            'avoid_when': ['Small datasets', 'No relationship expected'],
            'examples': ['Price vs sales', 'Age vs spending'],
            'variations': ['bubble', 'hexbin', 'regression']
        },
        'bubble': {
            'purpose': 'Show three-dimensional data',
            'best_for': ['Multi-variable comparison', 'Highlighting magnitude'],
            'avoid_when': ['Many bubbles', 'Precise comparison needed'],
            'examples': ['Risk vs return vs market cap'],
            'variations': ['animated', 'grouped']
        },
        'heatmap': {
            'purpose': 'Show magnitude with color',
            'best_for': ['Correlation matrices', 'Time patterns', 'Categorical relationships'],
            'avoid_when': ['Continuous data', 'Precise values needed'],
            'examples': ['Hourly activity by day', 'Correlation matrix'],
            'variations': ['annotated', 'clustered']
        },
        
        # Geospatial
        'choropleth': {
            'purpose': 'Show geographic patterns',
            'best_for': ['Regional data', 'Demographics', 'Election results'],
            'avoid_when': ['Small regions', 'Absolute values vary greatly'],
            'examples': ['Sales by state', 'Population density'],
            'variations': ['cartogram', 'bubble map']
        },
        'bubble_map': {
            'purpose': 'Show location and magnitude',
            'best_for': ['Point data', 'Magnitude comparison', 'Clustering'],
            'avoid_when': ['Too many points', 'Precise location needed'],
            'examples': ['Store locations', 'Earthquake magnitude'],
            'variations': ['animated', 'clustered']
        },
        
        # Advanced
        'sankey': {
            'purpose': 'Show flow and relationships',
            'best_for': ['User flows', 'Budget flows', 'Material flows'],
            'avoid_when': ['Simple relationships', 'Small datasets'],
            'examples': ['User journey', 'Energy flow', 'Budget allocation'],
            'variations': ['alluvial', 'chord diagram']
        },
        'funnel': {
            'purpose': 'Show progression through stages',
            'best_for': ['Conversion funnels', 'Process stages', 'Drop-off analysis'],
            'avoid_when': ['Not sequential', 'No drop-offs'],
            'examples': ['Sales funnel', 'Hiring process', 'Checkout flow'],
            'variations': ['horizontal', 'vertical', 'pyramid']
        },
        'gauge': {
            'purpose': 'Show progress toward goal',
            'best_for': ['KPI tracking', 'Performance vs target'],
            'avoid_when': ['Multiple metrics', 'Comparisons'],
            'examples': ['Sales vs quota', 'Server utilization'],
            'variations': ['bullet', 'thermometer']
        }
    }
    
    @classmethod
    def recommend_chart(cls, data_type, goal, audience='technical', constraints=None):
        """
        Recommend appropriate chart type based on requirements
        """
        recommendations = []
        
        # Data type mappings
        if data_type == 'comparison':
            if goal == 'ranking':
                recommendations.append(('bar', 'horizontal bar for easy label reading'))
            elif goal == 'time_trend':
                recommendations.append(('line', 'best for continuous time trends'))
                recommendations.append(('column', 'good for discrete time periods'))
            elif goal == 'group_comparison':
                recommendations.append(('grouped_bar', 'for comparing sub-categories'))
                recommendations.append(('grouped_column', 'for time-based groups'))
                
        elif data_type == 'distribution':
            if goal == 'summary_statistics':
                recommendations.append(('box_plot', 'for statistical summary'))
            elif goal == 'shape':
                recommendations.append(('histogram', 'for frequency distribution'))
                recommendations.append(('violin', 'for detailed shape and density'))
            elif goal == 'outliers':
                recommendations.append(('box_plot', 'clearly shows outliers'))
                recommendations.append(('scatter', 'for bivariate outliers'))
                
        elif data_type == 'proportion':
            if goal == 'part_to_whole':
                recommendations.append(('pie', 'only for 3-5 categories'))
                recommendations.append(('stacked_bar', 'better for more categories'))
            elif goal == 'hierarchy':
                recommendations.append(('treemap', 'for nested proportions'))
                recommendations.append(('sunburst', 'for multi-level hierarchies'))
                
        elif data_type == 'relationship':
            if goal == 'correlation':
                recommendations.append(('scatter', 'for bivariate correlation'))
                recommendations.append(('heatmap', 'for correlation matrices'))
            elif goal == 'multi_variable':
                recommendations.append(('bubble', 'for 3+ variables'))
                recommendations.append(('parallel_coordinates', 'for many dimensions'))
                
        elif data_type == 'geospatial':
            if goal == 'regional_comparison':
                recommendations.append(('choropleth', 'for areal data'))
            elif goal == 'point_data':
                recommendations.append(('bubble_map', 'for location + magnitude'))
                
        elif data_type == 'flow':
            if goal == 'process':
                recommendations.append(('funnel', 'for sequential stages'))
                recommendations.append(('sankey', 'for complex flows'))
        
        # Filter for audience
        if audience == 'executive':
            # Simplify for executives
            recommendations = [
                (chart, desc) for chart, desc in recommendations
                if chart not in ['box_plot', 'violin', 'sankey']
            ]
        
        return {
            'recommendations': recommendations,
            'considerations': cls._get_considerations(recommendations[0][0] if recommendations else None, constraints)
        }
    
    @classmethod
    def _get_considerations(cls, chart_type, constraints):
        """Get specific considerations for chart type"""
        if not chart_type or chart_type not in cls.CHART_TYPES:
            return []
        
        chart_info = cls.CHART_TYPES[chart_type]
        considerations = []
        
        considerations.append(f"Best for: {', '.join(chart_info['best_for'])}")
        considerations.append(f"Avoid when: {', '.join(chart_info['avoid_when'])}")
        
        if constraints:
            if constraints.get('small_screen'):
                considerations.append("Consider simplifying for small screens")
            if constraints.get('color_blind'):
                considerations.append("Use patterns or labels in addition to color")
            if constraints.get('print'):
                considerations.append("Ensure contrast works in black and white")
        
        return considerations

# Example usage
"""
recommendation = ChartSelectionGuide.recommend_chart(
    data_type='comparison',
    goal='time_trend',
    audience='executive',
    constraints={'small_screen': True}
)
print(recommendation)
"""
```

### Color Theory for Dashboards

```python
class DashboardColorPalette:
    """
    Professional color palettes for data visualization
    """
    
    # Sequential palettes (for ordered data)
    SEQUENTIAL = {
        'blue': ['#f7fbff', '#deebf7', '#c6dbef', '#9ecae1', '#6baed6', '#4292c6', '#2171b5', '#08519c', '#08306b'],
        'green': ['#f7fcf5', '#e5f5e0', '#c7e9c0', '#a1d99b', '#74c476', '#41ab5d', '#238b45', '#006d2c', '#00441b'],
        'orange': ['#fff5eb', '#fee6ce', '#fdd0a2', '#fdae6b', '#fd8d3c', '#f16913', '#d94801', '#a63603', '#7f2704'],
        'purple': ['#fcfbfd', '#efedf5', '#dadaeb', '#bcbddc', '#9e9ac8', '#807dba', '#6a51a3', '#54278f', '#3f007d'],
        'grayscale': ['#ffffff', '#f0f0f0', '#d9d9d9', '#bdbdbd', '#969696', '#737373', '#525252', '#252525', '#000000']
    }
    
    # Diverging palettes (for data with critical midpoint)
    DIVERGING = {
        'red_blue': ['#67001f', '#b2182b', '#d6604d', '#f4a582', '#fddbc7', '#f7f7f7', '#d1e5f0', '#92c5de', '#4393c3', '#2166ac', '#053061'],
        'red_green': ['#a50026', '#d73027', '#f46d43', '#fdae61', '#fee08b', '#ffffbf', '#d9ef8b', '#a6d96a', '#66bd63', '#1a9850', '#006837'],
        'purple_orange': ['#7f3b08', '#b35806', '#e08214', '#fdb863', '#fee0b6', '#f7f7f7', '#d8daeb', '#b2abd2', '#8073ac', '#542788', '#2d004b'],
        'brown_teal': ['#543005', '#8c510a', '#bf812d', '#dfc27d', '#f6e8c3', '#f5f5f5', '#c7eae5', '#80cdc1', '#35978f', '#01665e', '#003c30']
    }
    
    # Categorical palettes (for discrete categories)
    CATEGORICAL = {
        'vibrant': ['#e41a1c', '#377eb8', '#4daf4a', '#984ea3', '#ff7f00', '#ffff33', '#a65628', '#f781bf', '#999999'],
        'muted': ['#66c2a5', '#fc8d62', '#8da0cb', '#e78ac3', '#a6d854', '#ffd92f', '#e5c494', '#b3b3b3'],
        'accessible': ['#1170aa', '#fc7d0b', '#a3acb9', '#57606c', '#5fa2ce', '#c85200', '#7b848f', '#a3cce9', '#ffbc79', '#c8d0d9'],
        'colorblind_safe': ['#0072B2', '#E69F00', '#56B4E9', '#009E73', '#F0E442', '#D55E00', '#CC79A7', '#999999'],
        'corporate': ['#003f5c', '#2f4b7c', '#665191', '#a05195', '#d45087', '#f95d6a', '#ff7c43', '#ffa600']
    }
    
    # Semantic colors (for specific meanings)
    SEMANTIC = {
        'success': '#28a745',
        'warning': '#ffc107',
        'danger': '#dc3545',
        'info': '#17a2b8',
        'neutral': '#6c757d',
        'primary': '#007bff',
        'secondary': '#6c757d',
        'light': '#f8f9fa',
        'dark': '#343a40'
    }
    
    # Chart-specific colors
    CHART_COLORS = {
        'line_chart': ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c', '#d62728', '#9467bd', '#8c564b', '#e377c2', '#7f7f7f'],
        'bar_chart': ['#3182bd', '#6baed6', '#9ecae1', '#c6dbef', '#e6550d', '#fd8d3c', '#fdae6b', '#fdd0a2'],
        'heatmap': ['#440154', '#31688e', '#35b779', '#fde725'],  # Viridis-like
        'gauge': {
            'low': '#dc3545',
            'medium': '#ffc107',
            'high': '#28a745'
        }
    }
    
    @classmethod
    def get_sequential(cls, base_color='blue', n_colors=5):
        """Get sequential color palette"""
        palette = cls.SEQUENTIAL.get(base_color, cls.SEQUENTIAL['blue'])
        step = len(palette) // n_colors
        return [palette[i * step] for i in range(n_colors)]
    
    @classmethod
    def get_diverging(cls, palette_name='red_blue', n_colors=7):
        """Get diverging color palette"""
        palette = cls.DIVERGING.get(palette_name, cls.DIVERGING['red_blue'])
        step = max(1, len(palette) // n_colors)
        return [palette[i] for i in range(0, len(palette), step)][:n_colors]
    
    @classmethod
    def get_categorical(cls, palette_name='vibrant', n_colors=None):
        """Get categorical color palette"""
        palette = cls.CATEGORICAL.get(palette_name, cls.CATEGORICAL['vibrant'])
        if n_colors:
            return palette[:n_colors]
        return palette
    
    @classmethod
    def generate_custom_palette(cls, base_color, n_colors, variation='lightness'):
        """
        Generate custom color palette
        
        Args:
            base_color: Hex color code
            n_colors: Number of colors needed
            variation: 'lightness', 'saturation', or 'hue'
        """
        from colorsys import rgb_to_hls, hls_to_rgb, hex_to_rgb, rgb_to_hex
        
        # Convert hex to RGB
        base_rgb = tuple(int(base_color.lstrip('#')[i:i+2], 16) / 255.0 for i in (0, 2, 4))
        
        # Convert to HLS
        h, l, s = rgb_to_hls(*base_rgb)
        
        colors = []
        for i in range(n_colors):
            factor = i / (n_colors - 1) if n_colors > 1 else 0.5
            
            if variation == 'lightness':
                new_l = 0.1 + (factor * 0.8)  # From dark to light
                new_h, new_s = h, s
            elif variation == 'saturation':
                new_s = 0.2 + (factor * 0.8)
                new_h, new_l = h, l
            elif variation == 'hue':
                new_h = (h + factor * 0.5) % 1.0  # Shift hue
                new_s, new_l = s, l
            
            new_rgb = hls_to_rgb(new_h, new_l, new_s)
            new_hex = '#{:02x}{:02x}{:02x}'.format(
                int(new_rgb[0] * 255),
                int(new_rgb[1] * 255),
                int(new_rgb[2] * 255)
            )
            colors.append(new_hex)
        
        return colors
    
    @staticmethod
    def ensure_contrast(background_color, text_color):
        """
        Check and ensure sufficient contrast for accessibility
        Returns contrast ratio (should be >= 4.5 for normal text)
        """
        def get_luminance(hex_color):
            rgb = tuple(int(hex_color.lstrip('#')[i:i+2], 16) / 255.0 for i in (0, 2, 4))
            
            def adjust(c):
                if c <= 0.03928:
                    return c / 12.92
                return pow((c + 0.055) / 1.055, 2.4)
            
            r, g, b = [adjust(c) for c in rgb]
            return 0.2126 * r + 0.7152 * g + 0.0722 * b
        
        lum1 = get_luminance(background_color)
        lum2 = get_luminance(text_color)
        
        brightest = max(lum1, lum2)
        darkest = min(lum1, lum2)
        
        contrast = (brightest + 0.05) / (darkest + 0.05)
        
        return {
            'contrast_ratio': round(contrast, 2),
            'passes_wcag_aa': contrast >= 4.5,
            'passes_wcag_aaa': contrast >= 7,
            'passes_large_text_aa': contrast >= 3,
            'recommendation': 'Good contrast' if contrast >= 4.5 else 'Insufficient contrast - adjust colors'
        }

# Example usage with Plotly

import plotly.graph_objects as go

# Get color palette
colors = DashboardColorPalette.get_categorical('accessible', 5)

# Create chart with consistent colors
fig = go.Figure()
for i, category in enumerate(categories):
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name=category,
        x=x_values,
        y=y_values[i],
        marker_color=colors[i]
    ))

# Apply consistent styling
fig.update_layout(
    template='plotly_white',
    colorway=colors,
    font=dict(family='Arial', size=12),
    title_font=dict(size=16, color='#333333'),
    legend=dict(
        orientation='h',
        yanchor='bottom',
        y=1.02,
        xanchor='right',
        x=1
    )
)
"""
```

### Dashboard UX Principles

```python
class DashboardUX:
    """
    Dashboard user experience best practices
    """
    
    PRINCIPLES = {
        'clarity': {
            'title': 'Clarity First',
            'rules': [
                'Use descriptive titles and labels',
                'Avoid jargon and acronyms',
                'Provide clear units of measurement',
                'Use consistent terminology',
                'Include data source information'
            ]
        },
        'hierarchy': {
            'title': 'Visual Hierarchy',
            'rules': [
                'Most important metrics in top-left',
                'Use size to indicate importance',
                'Group related metrics together',
                'Use whitespace effectively',
                'Limit number of metrics per view (5-9)'
            ]
        },
        'consistency': {
            'title': 'Design Consistency',
            'rules': [
                'Same metric format across dashboard',
                'Consistent color usage',
                'Uniform date/time formatting',
                'Standardized number formatting',
                'Consistent chart types for similar data'
            ]
        },
        'responsiveness': {
            'title': 'Responsive Design',
            'rules': [
                'Optimize for target screen size',
                'Consider mobile viewing',
                'Use scalable chart libraries',
                'Test on different devices',
                'Prioritize metrics for small screens'
            ]
        },
        'interactivity': {
            'title': 'Thoughtful Interactivity',
            'rules': [
                'Hover tooltips with context',
                'Drill-down for detailed views',
                'Cross-filtering between charts',
                'Date range selectors',
                'Reset filters easily'
            ]
        },
        'performance': {
            'title': 'Performance Optimization',
            'rules': [
                'Load time under 3 seconds',
                'Lazy load below-fold content',
                'Cache frequently accessed data',
                'Optimize query performance',
                'Show loading states'
            ]
        }
    }
    
    LAYOUT_PATTERNS = {
        'f_pattern': {
            'description': 'F-shaped reading pattern',
            'best_for': 'Text-heavy dashboards',
            'layout': '''
            [KPI 1] [KPI 2] [KPI 3] [KPI 4]
            [Main Chart ...................]
            [Secondary 1] [Secondary 2]
            [Data Table ...................]
            '''
        },
        'z_pattern': {
            'description': 'Z-shaped visual flow',
            'best_for': 'Visual dashboards',
            'layout': '''
            [Most Important Metric]
            [Supporting Charts    ]
            [Additional Context   ]
            [Detailed Data Table  ]
            '''
        },
        'dashboard_types': {
            'strategic': {
                'update_frequency': 'Daily/Weekly',
                'audience': 'Executives',
                'focus': 'High-level KPIs',
                'chart_complexity': 'Low',
                'example_layout': '''
                [Scorecard Row: Revenue, Users, Conversion, LTV]
                [Trend Chart: Performance Over Time]
                [Comparison: Actual vs Target]
                '''
            },
            'analytical': {
                'update_frequency': 'Hourly/Daily',
                'audience': 'Analysts',
                'focus': 'Detailed insights',
                'chart_complexity': 'High',
                'example_layout': '''
                [Filters and Date Range]
                [Multi-metric Dashboard]
                [Drill-down Tables]
                [Export Options]
                '''
            },
            'operational': {
                'update_frequency': 'Real-time',
                'audience': 'Operations teams',
                'focus': 'Immediate action items',
                'chart_complexity': 'Medium',
                'example_layout': '''
                [Alert Status Indicators]
                [Real-time Metrics]
                [Status Tables]
                [Action Buttons]
                '''
            }
        }
    }
    
    @classmethod
    def apply_best_practices(cls, dashboard_config):
        """
        Apply UX best practices to dashboard configuration
        """
        recommendations = []
        
        # Check title clarity
        if not dashboard_config.get('title'):
            recommendations.append({
                'category': 'clarity',
                'issue': 'Missing dashboard title',
                'recommendation': 'Add descriptive title that states purpose'
            })
        
        # Check metric count
        metrics = dashboard_config.get('metrics', [])
        if len(metrics) > 9:
            recommendations.append({
                'category': 'hierarchy',
                'issue': f'Too many metrics ({len(metrics)})',
                'recommendation': 'Limit to 5-9 key metrics per view, create tabs for more'
            })
        
        # Check color consistency
        colors_used = set()
        for chart in dashboard_config.get('charts', []):
            colors_used.update(chart.get('colors', []))
        
        if len(colors_used) > 10:
            recommendations.append({
                'category': 'consistency',
                'issue': 'Too many different colors',
                'recommendation': 'Use consistent color palette across charts'
            })
        
        # Check for context
        if not dashboard_config.get('last_updated'):
            recommendations.append({
                'category': 'clarity',
                'issue': 'Missing data freshness indicator',
                'recommendation': 'Add last updated timestamp'
            })
        
        return {
            'score': max(0, 100 - len(recommendations) * 10),
            'recommendations': recommendations,
            'passed_principles': [p for p in cls.PRINCIPLES.keys() if p not in [r['category'] for r in recommendations]]
        }

# Implementing with modern dashboard libraries
"""
# Streamlit example with UX best practices
import streamlit as st

def create_executive_dashboard(data):
    # Apply F-pattern layout
    st.title("📊 Executive Performance Dashboard")
    st.caption(f"Last updated: {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')}")
    
    # Row 1: Key metrics (F-pattern start)
    col1, col2, col3, col4 = st.columns(4)
    
    with col1:
        st.metric(
            label="Total Revenue",
            value=f"${data['revenue']:,.0f}",
            delta=f"{data['revenue_change']:.1f}%"
        )
    
    with col2:
        st.metric(
            label="Active Users",
            value=f"{data['users']:,.0f}",
            delta=f"{data['users_change']:.1f}%"
        )
    
    with col3:
        st.metric(
            label="Conversion Rate",
            value=f"{data['conversion']:.2f}%",
            delta=f"{data['conversion_change']:.1f}%"
        )
    
    with col4:
        st.metric(
            label="Customer LTV",
            value=f"${data['ltv']:,.0f}",
            delta=f"{data['ltv_change']:.1f}%"
        )
    
    # Row 2: Main trend chart
    st.subheader("Performance Trend")
    st.line_chart(data['trend_data'])
    
    # Row 3: Breakdown charts
    col1, col2 = st.columns(2)
    
    with col1:
        st.subheader("By Channel")
        st.bar_chart(data['channel_data'])
    
    with col2:
        st.subheader("By Region")
        st.bar_chart(data['region_data'])
    
    # Row 4: Detailed data table
    st.subheader("Detailed Breakdown")
    st.dataframe(data['detailed_data'], use_container_width=True)
"""
```

### D3.js Custom Visualizations

```javascript
// D3.js Custom Visualization Components

// 1. Animated Progress Gauge
class GaugeChart {
    constructor(containerId, config) {
        this.container = d3.select(`#${containerId}`);
        this.width = config.width || 300;
        this.height = config.height || 300;
        this.value = config.value || 0;
        this.min = config.min || 0;
        this.max = config.max || 100;
        this.label = config.label || '';
        
        this.init();
    }
    
    init() {
        const margin = { top: 20, right: 20, bottom: 30, left: 20 };
        this.innerWidth = this.width - margin.left - margin.right;
        this.innerHeight = this.height - margin.top - margin.bottom;
        this.radius = Math.min(this.innerWidth, this.innerHeight) / 2;
        
        this.svg = this.container
            .append('svg')
            .attr('width', this.width)
            .attr('height', this.height);
        
        this.g = this.svg
            .append('g')
            .attr('transform', `translate(${this.width / 2}, ${this.height * 0.8})`);
        
        this.drawArcs();
        this.drawNeedle();
        this.drawLabels();
        this.animateToValue(this.value);
    }
    
    drawArcs() {
        const arcGenerator = d3.arc()
            .innerRadius(this.radius * 0.6)
            .outerRadius(this.radius)
            .cornerRadius(5);
        
        // Background arc
        this.g.append('path')
            .datum({
                startAngle: -Math.PI / 2,
                endAngle: Math.PI / 2
            })
            .attr('d', arcGenerator)
            .attr('fill', '#e0e0e0');
        
        // Colored segments
        const colorScale = d3.scaleLinear()
            .domain([0, 50, 100])
            .range(['#ff4444', '#ffbb33', '#00C851']);
        
        const segments = [
            { start: -90, end: -30, color: '#ff4444' },
            { start: -30, end: 30, color: '#ffbb33' },
            { start: 30, end: 90, color: '#00C851' }
        ];
        
        segments.forEach(seg => {
            this.g.append('path')
                .datum({
                    startAngle: (seg.start * Math.PI) / 180,
                    endAngle: (seg.end * Math.PI) / 180
                })
                .attr('d', arcGenerator)
                .attr('fill', seg.color)
                .attr('opacity', 0.8);
        });
    }
    
    drawNeedle() {
        this.needle = this.g.append('line')
            .attr('class', 'needle')
            .attr('x1', 0)
            .attr('y1', 0)
            .attr('x2', 0)
            .attr('y2', -this.radius * 0.9)
            .attr('stroke', '#333')
            .attr('stroke-width', 4)
            .attr('stroke-linecap', 'round')
            .attr('transform', 'rotate(-90)');
        
        // Center dot
        this.g.append('circle')
            .attr('cx', 0)
            .attr('cy', 0)
            .attr('r', 8)
            .attr('fill', '#333');
    }
    
    drawLabels() {
        // Value label
        this.valueLabel = this.g.append('text')
            .attr('class', 'value')
            .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
            .attr('y', -this.radius * 0.3)
            .attr('font-size', '36px')
            .attr('font-weight', 'bold')
            .attr('fill', '#333')
            .text('0');
        
        // Metric label
        this.g.append('text')
            .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
            .attr('y', 30)
            .attr('font-size', '14px')
            .attr('fill', '#666')
            .text(this.label);
        
        // Min/Max labels
        this.g.append('text')
            .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
            .attr('x', -this.radius)
            .attr('y', 15)
            .attr('font-size', '12px')
            .attr('fill', '#999')
            .text(this.min);
        
        this.g.append('text')
            .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
            .attr('x', this.radius)
            .attr('y', 15)
            .attr('font-size', '12px')
            .attr('fill', '#999')
            .text(this.max);
    }
    
    animateToValue(value) {
        const percentage = (value - this.min) / (this.max - this.min);
        const angle = -90 + (percentage * 180);
        
        this.needle
            .transition()
            .duration(1500)
            .ease(d3.easeElasticOut)
            .attr('transform', `rotate(${angle})`);
        
        this.valueLabel
            .transition()
            .duration(1500)
            .tween('text', function() {
                const that = d3.select(this);
                const i = d3.interpolateNumber(0, value);
                return function(t) {
                    that.text(Math.round(i(t)));
                };
            });
    }
}

// 2. Horizon Chart for Time Series
class HorizonChart {
    constructor(containerId, data, config) {
        this.container = d3.select(`#${containerId}`);
        this.data = data;
        this.width = config.width || 800;
        this.height = config.height || 200;
        this.bands = config.bands || 4;
        
        this.init();
    }
    
    init() {
        const margin = { top: 20, right: 20, bottom: 30, left: 50 };
        this.innerWidth = this.width - margin.left - margin.right;
        this.innerHeight = this.height - margin.top - margin.bottom;
        
        this.svg = this.container
            .append('svg')
            .attr('width', this.width)
            .attr('height', this.height);
        
        this.g = this.svg
            .append('g')
            .attr('transform', `translate(${margin.left},${margin.top})`);
        
        this.xScale = d3.scaleTime()
            .domain(d3.extent(this.data, d => d.date))
            .range([0, this.innerWidth]);
        
        this.yScale = d3.scaleLinear()
            .domain([0, d3.max(this.data, d => d.value)])
            .range([this.innerHeight, 0]);
        
        this.drawChart();
        this.drawAxes();
    }
    
    drawChart() {
        const step = d3.max(this.data, d => d.value) / this.bands;
        const colors = d3.schemeBlues[this.bands + 1].slice(1);
        
        const area = d3.area()
            .x(d => this.xScale(d.date))
            .y0(this.innerHeight)
            .y1(d => this.yScale(d.value))
            .curve(d3.curveBasis);
        
        // Draw bands
        for (let i = 0; i < this.bands; i++) {
            const bandData = this.data.map(d => ({
                date: d.date,
                value: Math.min(Math.max(d.value - i * step, 0), step)
            }));
            
            this.g.append('path')
                .datum(bandData)
                .attr('fill', colors[i])
                .attr('d', area)
                .attr('transform', `translate(0, ${-i * (this.innerHeight / this.bands)})`);
        }
    }
    
    drawAxes() {
        const xAxis = d3.axisBottom(this.xScale)
            .ticks(5)
            .tickFormat(d3.timeFormat('%b %d'));
        
        this.g.append('g')
            .attr('transform', `translate(0,${this.innerHeight})`)
            .call(xAxis);
        
        const yAxis = d3.axisLeft(this.yScale)
            .ticks(5);
        
        this.g.append('g')
            .call(yAxis);
    }
}

// 3. Radial Progress Chart
class RadialProgress {
    constructor(containerId, config) {
        this.container = d3.select(`#${containerId}`);
        this.size = config.size || 200;
        this.progress = config.progress || 0;
        this.strokeWidth = config.strokeWidth || 12;
        
        this.init();
    }
    
    init() {
        const radius = (this.size - this.strokeWidth) / 2;
        
        this.svg = this.container
            .append('svg')
            .attr('width', this.size)
            .attr('height', this.size);
        
        const g = this.svg.append('g')
            .attr('transform', `translate(${this.size / 2},${this.size / 2})`);
        
        // Background circle
        g.append('circle')
            .attr('r', radius)
            .attr('fill', 'none')
            .attr('stroke', '#e0e0e0')
            .attr('stroke-width', this.strokeWidth);
        
        // Progress arc
        const arc = d3.arc()
            .innerRadius(radius - this.strokeWidth / 2)
            .outerRadius(radius + this.strokeWidth / 2)
            .startAngle(0)
            .cornerRadius(this.strokeWidth / 2);
        
        this.progressArc = g.append('path')
            .datum({ endAngle: 0 })
            .attr('d', arc)
            .attr('fill', '#4CAF50');
        
        // Center text
        this.text = g.append('text')
            .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
            .attr('dy', '0.35em')
            .attr('font-size', '24px')
            .attr('font-weight', 'bold')
            .text('0%');
        
        this.animate(this.progress);
    }
    
    animate(targetProgress) {
        const endAngle = (targetProgress / 100) * 2 * Math.PI;
        
        this.progressArc
            .transition()
            .duration(1000)
            .attrTween('d', function(d) {
                const interpolate = d3.interpolate(d.endAngle, endAngle);
                return function(t) {
                    d.endAngle = interpolate(t);
                    return d3.arc()
                        .innerRadius(88)
                        .outerRadius(100)
                        .startAngle(0)
                        .cornerRadius(6)(d);
                };
            });
        
        this.text
            .transition()
            .duration(1000)
            .tween('text', function() {
                const that = d3.select(this);
                const i = d3.interpolateNumber(0, targetProgress);
                return function(t) {
                    that.text(Math.round(i(t)) + '%');
                };
            });
    }
}

// Usage Example:
"""
// HTML: <div id="gauge"></div>
// <div id="horizon"></div>
// <div id="radial"></div>

// Initialize charts
const gauge = new GaugeChart('gauge', {
    width: 300,
    height: 300,
    value: 75,
    min: 0,
    max: 100,
    label: 'Performance Score'
});

const horizonData = [
    { date: new Date('2024-01-01'), value: 30 },
    { date: new Date('2024-01-02'), value: 45 },
    // ... more data
];

const horizon = new HorizonChart('horizon', horizonData, {
    width: 800,
    height: 150,
    bands: 4
});

const radial = new RadialProgress('radial', {
    size: 150,
    progress: 67,
    strokeWidth: 10
});
"""
```

### Storytelling with Data

```python
class DataStorytelling:
    """
    Framework for creating compelling data narratives
    """
    
    NARRATIVE_STRUCTURES = {
        'comparison': {
            'pattern': 'Before → After → Insight',
            'best_for': 'Demonstrating change or impact',
            'example_flow': [
                'Show baseline (before state)',
                'Show current state (after)',
                'Highlight the difference',
                'Explain what caused the change',
                'Implications for business'
            ]
        },
        'conflict_resolution': {
            'pattern': 'Problem → Analysis → Solution',
            'best_for': 'Troubleshooting or optimization',
            'example_flow': [
                'Identify the problem clearly',
                'Present supporting data',
                'Analyze root causes',
                'Propose solutions',
                'Show expected outcomes'
            ]
        },
        'journey': {
            'pattern': 'Start → Progress → Destination',
            'best_for': 'User journeys or processes',
            'example_flow': [
                'Set the scene (starting point)',
                'Show key milestones',
                'Highlight challenges or wins',
                'Reach conclusion',
                'Call to action'
            ]
        },
        'exploration': {
            'pattern': 'Question → Investigation → Answer',
            'best_for': 'Research or discovery',
            'example_flow': [
                'Pose the question',
                'Present the investigation',
                'Reveal key findings',
                'Interpret the results',
                'Suggest next steps'
            ]
        }
    }
    
    @classmethod
    def create_story(cls, data, structure_type='comparison', audience='executive'):
        """
        Create a data story structure
        """
        structure = cls.NARRATIVE_STRUCTURES.get(structure_type)
        
        if not structure:
            return {'error': 'Invalid structure type'}
        
        story = {
            'title': cls._generate_title(data, structure_type),
            'structure': structure['pattern'],
            'scenes': [],
            'key_insights': [],
            'recommendations': []
        }
        
        # Build scenes based on structure
        if structure_type == 'comparison':
            story['scenes'] = [
                {
                    'type': 'metric',
                    'title': 'Starting Point',
                    'content': data.get('baseline'),
                    'visualization': 'scorecard'
                },
                {
                    'type': 'metric',
                    'title': 'Current State',
                    'content': data.get('current'),
                    'visualization': 'scorecard_with_delta'
                },
                {
                    'type': 'chart',
                    'title': 'The Change Over Time',
                    'content': data.get('trend_data'),
                    'visualization': 'line_chart'
                },
                {
                    'type': 'breakdown',
                    'title': 'Key Drivers',
                    'content': data.get('drivers'),
                    'visualization': 'waterfall_chart'
                }
            ]
        
        elif structure_type == 'journey':
            story['scenes'] = [
                {
                    'type': 'funnel',
                    'title': 'User Journey Overview',
                    'content': data.get('funnel_data'),
                    'visualization': 'funnel_chart'
                },
                {
                    'type': 'heatmap',
                    'title': 'Drop-off Points',
                    'content': data.get('dropoff_analysis'),
                    'visualization': 'heatmap'
                },
                {
                    'type': 'comparison',
                    'title': 'Successful vs Abandoned',
                    'content': data.get('comparison_data'),
                    'visualization': 'side_by_side'
                }
            ]
        
        # Extract key insights
        story['key_insights'] = cls._extract_insights(data, structure_type)
        
        # Generate recommendations
        story['recommendations'] = cls._generate_recommendations(data, structure_type)
        
        # Adjust for audience
        if audience == 'executive':
            story = cls._simplify_for_executives(story)
        
        return story
    
    @staticmethod
    def _generate_title(data, structure_type):
        """Generate compelling title based on data"""
        titles = {
            'comparison': f"Revenue Growth: {data.get('growth_pct', 0):.1f}% Increase",
            'conflict_resolution': f"Identified and Resolved: {data.get('issue', 'Key Issue')}",
            'journey': f"Optimizing the {data.get('journey_name', 'Customer')} Journey",
            'exploration': f"Uncovering Opportunities in {data.get('topic', 'Key Area')}"
        }
        return titles.get(structure_type, 'Data Analysis Report')
    
    @staticmethod
    def _extract_insights(data, structure_type):
        """Extract automated insights from data"""
        insights = []
        
        # Trend insight
        if 'trend_data' in data:
            trend = data['trend_data']
            if len(trend) >= 2:
                change = ((trend[-1] - trend[0]) / trend[0] * 100) if trend[0] != 0 else 0
                direction = 'increased' if change > 0 else 'decreased'
                insights.append(f"Metric has {direction} by {abs(change):.1f}% over the period")
        
        # Outlier insight
        if 'outliers' in data and data['outliers']:
            insights.append(f"Identified {len(data['outliers'])} significant outliers requiring attention")
        
        # Correlation insight
        if 'correlation' in data:
            corr = data['correlation']
            strength = 'strong' if abs(corr) > 0.7 else 'moderate' if abs(corr) > 0.4 else 'weak'
            direction = 'positive' if corr > 0 else 'negative'
            insights.append(f"{strength.capitalize()} {direction} correlation detected between variables")
        
        return insights
    
    @staticmethod
    def _generate_recommendations(data, structure_type):
        """Generate actionable recommendations"""
        recommendations = []
        
        if structure_type == 'comparison' and data.get('growth_pct', 0) < 0:
            recommendations.append({
                'priority': 'high',
                'action': 'Investigate decline drivers immediately',
                'owner': 'Analytics Team',
                'timeline': 'This week'
            })
        
        if structure_type == 'journey':
            if data.get('dropoff_rate', 0) > 0.5:
                recommendations.append({
                    'priority': 'high',
                    'action': 'Address major drop-off point in user journey',
                    'owner': 'Product Team',
                    'timeline': 'Next sprint'
                })
        
        return recommendations
    
    @staticmethod
    def _simplify_for_executives(story):
        """Simplify story for executive audience"""
        # Limit to 3 scenes
        story['scenes'] = story['scenes'][:3]
        
        # Simplify visualizations
        for scene in story['scenes']:
            if scene['visualization'] in ['waterfall_chart', 'funnel_chart']:
                scene['visualization'] = 'scorecard_with_trend'
        
        # Limit insights
        story['key_insights'] = story['key_insights'][:2]
        
        return story
    
    @classmethod
    def create_presentation_outline(cls, story):
        """
        Create presentation outline from story
        """
        outline = {
            'title': story['title'],
            'slides': []
        }
        
        # Title slide
        outline['slides'].append({
            'type': 'title',
            'content': story['title'],
            'notes': 'Set context and expectations'
        })
        
        # Agendas/scenes
        for i, scene in enumerate(story['scenes'], 1):
            outline['slides'].append({
                'type': 'content',
                'title': scene['title'],
                'visualization': scene['visualization'],
                'speaker_notes': f"Key points for scene {i}"
            })
        
        # Insights slide
        outline['slides'].append({
            'type': 'summary',
            'title': 'Key Insights',
            'bullets': story['key_insights'],
            'speaker_notes': 'Synthesize the story'
        })
        
        # Recommendations slide
        outline['slides'].append({
            'type': 'action',
            'title': 'Recommendations',
            'content': story['recommendations'],
            'speaker_notes': 'Clear call to action'
        })
        
        return outline

# Example usage
"""
data = {
    'baseline': 100000,
    'current': 125000,
    'growth_pct': 25,
    'trend_data': [100, 105, 110, 115, 125],
    'drivers': {
        'marketing': 15,
        'product': 8,
        'pricing': 2
    }
}

story = DataStorytelling.create_story(data, structure_type='comparison', audience='executive')
presentation = DataStorytelling.create_presentation_outline(story)

print(json.dumps(story, indent=2))
```

## Report Templates

### Executive Summary Formats

```python
class ExecutiveSummaryTemplates:
    """
    Professional executive summary templates
    """
    
    TEMPLATES = {
        'financial_performance': {
            'title': 'Financial Performance Summary',
            'sections': [
                {
                    'name': 'Key Metrics',
                    'format': 'scorecard_row',
                    'metrics': [
                        {'label': 'Revenue', 'format': 'currency', 'comparison': True},
                        {'label': 'Profit', 'format': 'currency', 'comparison': True},
                        {'label': 'Margin', 'format': 'percentage', 'comparison': True},
                        {'label': 'ROI', 'format': 'percentage', 'comparison': True}
                    ]
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Performance Highlights',
                    'format': 'bullet_list',
                    'max_items': 5
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Concerns',
                    'format': 'bullet_list',
                    'max_items': 3
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Action Items',
                    'format': 'action_table',
                    'columns': ['Item', 'Owner', 'Due Date', 'Status']
                }
            ]
        },
        'marketing_dashboard': {
            'title': 'Marketing Performance Summary',
            'sections': [
                {
                    'name': 'Campaign Overview',
                    'format': 'scorecard_row',
                    'metrics': [
                        {'label': 'Spend', 'format': 'currency'},
                        {'label': 'Impressions', 'format': 'number'},
                        {'label': 'Clicks', 'format': 'number'},
                        {'label': 'Conversions', 'format': 'number'},
                        {'label': 'CPA', 'format': 'currency'}
                    ]
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Top Performing Channels',
                    'format': 'ranked_table',
                    'columns': ['Channel', 'Spend', 'Revenue', 'ROAS'],
                    'limit': 5
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Recommendations',
                    'format': 'priority_list',
                    'items': [
                        {'priority': 'high', 'text': 'Increase budget on top performers'},
                        {'priority': 'medium', 'text': 'Optimize underperforming campaigns'},
                        {'priority': 'low', 'text': 'Test new creative variants'}
                    ]
                }
            ]
        },
        'operational_review': {
            'title': 'Operational Performance Review',
            'sections': [
                {
                    'name': 'KPI Status',
                    'format': 'status_dashboard',
                    'kpis': [
                        {'name': 'Uptime', 'target': 99.9, 'actual': 99.95, 'unit': '%'},
                        {'name': 'Response Time', 'target': 200, 'actual': 180, 'unit': 'ms'},
                        {'name': 'Error Rate', 'target': 0.1, 'actual': 0.05, 'unit': '%'}
                    ]
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Incident Summary',
                    'format': 'incident_table',
                    'columns': ['Date', 'Severity', 'Description', 'Resolution Time', 'Status']
                },
                {
                    'name': 'Resource Utilization',
                    'format': 'gauge_chart',
                    'metrics': ['CPU', 'Memory', 'Storage', 'Bandwidth']
                }
            ]
        }
    }
    
    @classmethod
    def generate_executive_summary(cls, template_name, data):
        """
        Generate executive summary from template
        """
        template = cls.TEMPLATES.get(template_name)
        if not template:
            return {'error': f'Template {template_name} not found'}
        
        summary = {
            'title': template['title'],
            'date_generated': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            'prepared_for': data.get('audience', 'Executive Team'),
            'sections': []
        }
        
        for section in template['sections']:
            section_data = {
                'name': section['name'],
                'format': section['format'],
                'content': cls._render_section(section, data)
            }
            summary['sections'].append(section_data)
        
        return summary
    
    @classmethod
    def _render_section(cls, section_config, data):
        """Render individual section"""
        format_type = section_config['format']
        
        if format_type == 'scorecard_row':
            return cls._render_scorecard(section_config['metrics'], data)
        elif format_type == 'bullet_list':
            return data.get(section_config['name'].lower().replace(' ', '_'), [])
        elif format_type == 'action_table':
            return data.get('action_items', [])
        elif format_type == 'ranked_table':
            return cls._render_ranked_table(
                section_config['columns'],
                data.get('ranked_data', []),
                section_config.get('limit', 10)
            )
        elif format_type == 'status_dashboard':
            return cls._render_status_dashboard(section_config['kpis'])
        else:
            return data.get(section_config['name'].lower().replace(' ', '_'), {})
    
    @staticmethod
    def _render_scorecard(metrics, data):
        """Render scorecard metrics"""
        scorecards = []
        for metric in metrics:
            value = data.get(metric['label'].lower().replace(' ', '_'), 0)
            scorecards.append({
                'label': metric['label'],
                'value': value,
                'format': metric['format'],
                'comparison': metric.get('comparison', False)
            })
        return scorecards
    
    @staticmethod
    def _render_ranked_table(columns, data, limit):
        """Render ranked table"""
        sorted_data = sorted(data, key=lambda x: x.get(columns[-1], 0), reverse=True)
        return {
            'columns': columns,
            'data': sorted_data[:limit]
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def _render_status_dashboard(kpis):
        """Render status dashboard with color coding"""
        dashboard = []
        for kpi in kpis:
            actual = kpi['actual']
            target = kpi['target']
            
            # Determine status
            if kpi['name'] in ['Uptime', 'Response Time']:  # Higher is better
                performance = (actual / target - 1) * 100
            else:  # Lower is better
                performance = (1 - actual / target) * 100
            
            if performance >= 0:
                status = 'exceeds'
                color = 'green'
            elif performance >= -10:
                status = 'on_track'
                color = 'yellow'
            else:
                status = 'below_target'
                color = 'red'
            
            dashboard.append({
                'name': kpi['name'],
                'actual': actual,
                'target': target,
                'unit': kpi['unit'],
                'performance_vs_target': performance,
                'status': status,
                'color': color
            })
        
        return dashboard

# Automated Report Generation
class AutomatedReportGenerator:
    """
    Generate automated reports on schedule
    """
    
    def __init__(self, config):
        self.config = config
        self.output_dir = config.get('output_dir', './reports')
        self.templates_dir = config.get('templates_dir', './templates')
    
    def generate_weekly_report(self, week_start_date, data_sources):
        """
        Generate weekly performance report
        """
        report = {
            'type': 'weekly',
            'period': f"{week_start_date} to {(datetime.strptime(week_start_date, '%Y-%m-%d') + timedelta(days=6)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}",
            'generated_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            'sections': []
        }
        
        # 1. Executive Summary
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Executive Summary',
            'content': self._generate_weekly_summary(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 2. Traffic Overview
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Traffic Performance',
            'content': self._analyze_traffic(data_sources.get('ga4'))
        })
        
        # 3. Conversion Analysis
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Conversion Performance',
            'content': self._analyze_conversions(data_sources.get('ga4'))
        })
        
        # 4. Channel Performance
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Channel Breakdown',
            'content': self._analyze_channels(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 5. Top Content
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Top Performing Content',
            'content': self._analyze_content(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 6. Recommendations
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Recommendations',
            'content': self._generate_recommendations(report['sections'])
        })
        
        return report
    
    def generate_monthly_report(self, month, year, data_sources):
        """
        Generate comprehensive monthly report
        """
        report = {
            'type': 'monthly',
            'period': f"{year}-{month:02d}",
            'generated_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            'sections': []
        }
        
        # 1. Executive Summary with YoY comparison
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Executive Summary',
            'content': self._generate_monthly_summary(data_sources, month, year)
        })
        
        # 2. Detailed Traffic Analysis
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Traffic Analysis',
            'content': self._detailed_traffic_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 3. Funnel Analysis
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Conversion Funnel',
            'content': self._funnel_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 4. Cohort Analysis
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'User Retention',
            'content': self._cohort_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 5. Channel Attribution
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Attribution Analysis',
            'content': self._attribution_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 6. Goals vs Actual
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Performance vs Goals',
            'content': self._goals_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 7. Trend Analysis
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Trend Analysis',
            'content': self._trend_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 8. Action Items
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Strategic Recommendations',
            'content': self._strategic_recommendations(report['sections'])
        })
        
        return report
    
    def generate_quarterly_report(self, quarter, year, data_sources):
        """
        Generate strategic quarterly report
        """
        report = {
            'type': 'quarterly',
            'period': f"Q{quarter} {year}",
            'generated_at': datetime.now().isoformat(),
            'sections': []
        }
        
        # 1. Quarterly Executive Summary
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Quarterly Executive Summary',
            'content': self._generate_quarterly_summary(data_sources, quarter, year)
        })
        
        # 2. Business Performance Review
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Business Performance',
            'content': self._business_performance_review(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 3. Market Analysis
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Market Position',
            'content': self._market_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 4. Competitive Analysis
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Competitive Landscape',
            'content': self._competitive_analysis(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 5. Customer Insights
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Customer Insights',
            'content': self._customer_insights(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 6. Product Performance
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Product Performance',
            'content': self._product_performance(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 7. Forecast and Projections
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Forecast & Projections',
            'content': self._generate_forecasts(data_sources)
        })
        
        # 8. Strategic Initiatives
        report['sections'].append({
            'title': 'Strategic Initiatives',
            'content': self._strategic_initiatives(report['sections'])
        })
        
        return report
    
    def _generate_weekly_summary(self, data_sources):
        """Generate weekly summary content"""
        return {
            'key_metrics': {
                'traffic_change': '+5.2%',
                'conversion_change': '+2.1%',
                'revenue_change': '+8.7%'
            },
            'highlights': [
                'Organic traffic increased 12% due to content refresh',
                'Email campaigns delivered highest ROI this week',
                'Mobile conversion rate improved after checkout optimization'
            ],
            'concerns': [
                'Paid search CPC increased 15%',
                'Bounce rate on product pages remains high'
            ]
        }
    
    def export_report(self, report, format='pdf'):
        """
        Export report to specified format
        """
        filename = f"{report['type']}_report_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d')}.{format}"
        filepath = os.path.join(self.output_dir, filename)
        
        if format == 'pdf':
            return self._export_pdf(report, filepath)
        elif format == 'html':
            return self._export_html(report, filepath)
        elif format == 'pptx':
            return self._export_pptx(report, filepath)
        elif format == 'docx':
            return self._export_docx(report, filepath)
        else:
            return self._export_json(report, filepath.replace(f'.{format}', '.json'))
    
    def _export_pdf(self, report, filepath):
        """Export to PDF using ReportLab"""
        from reportlab.lib import colors
        from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
        from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Table, TableStyle, Paragraph, Spacer
        from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet
        
        doc = SimpleDocTemplate(filepath, pagesize=letter)
        styles = getSampleStyleSheet()
        story = []
        
        # Title
        story.append(Paragraph(report['type'].upper() + ' REPORT', styles['Title']))
        story.append(Paragraph(f"Period: {report['period']}", styles['Normal']))
        story.append(Spacer(1, 20))
        
        # Sections
        for section in report['sections']:
            story.append(Paragraph(section['title'], styles['Heading1']))
            story.append(Spacer(1, 10))
            
            # Add section content as table or text
            if isinstance(section['content'], dict):
                for key, value in section['content'].items():
                    story.append(Paragraph(f"<b>{key}:</b> {value}", styles['Normal']))
            
            story.append(Spacer(1, 20))
        
        doc.build(story)
        return filepath
    
    def _export_html(self, report, filepath):
        """Export to HTML"""
        html_content = f"""
        <!DOCTYPE html>
        <html>
        <head>
            <title>{report['type'].upper()} Report</title>
            <style>
                body {{ font-family: Arial, sans-serif; margin: 40px; }}
                h1 {{ color: #333; border-bottom: 2px solid #007bff; padding-bottom: 10px; }}
                h2 {{ color: #555; margin-top: 30px; }}
                .metric {{ background: #f8f9fa; padding: 15px; margin: 10px 0; border-radius: 5px; }}
                .positive {{ color: #28a745; }}
                .negative {{ color: #dc3545; }}
                table {{ border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin: 20px 0; }}
                th, td {{ border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 12px; text-align: left; }}
                th {{ background-color: #007bff; color: white; }}
            </style>
        </head>
        <body>
            <h1>{report['type'].upper()} REPORT</h1>
            <p><strong>Period:</strong> {report['period']}</p>
            <p><strong>Generated:</strong> {report['generated_at']}</p>
        """
        
        for section in report['sections']:
            html_content += f"<h2>{section['title']}</h2>"
            html_content += f"<div class='section-content'>{json.dumps(section['content'])}</div>"
        
        html_content += "</body></html>"
        
        with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
            f.write(html_content)
        
        return filepath

# Example: Automated weekly report
"""
generator = AutomatedReportGenerator({
    'output_dir': './reports',
    'templates_dir': './templates'
})

# Generate weekly report
data_sources = {
    'ga4': ga4_client,
    'gsc': gsc_service,
    'bigquery': bq_client
}

weekly_report = generator.generate_weekly_report(
    week_start_date='2024-01-01',
    data_sources=data_sources
)

# Export as PDF
pdf_path = generator.export_report(weekly_report, format='pdf')
print(f"Report generated: {pdf_path}")
"""
```

### Client vs Internal Report Templates

```python
class ReportTemplateManager:
    """
    Manage different report templates for different audiences
    """
    
    CLIENT_REPORT = {
        'audience': 'external_client',
        'tone': 'professional',
        'technical_level': 'low',
        'focus': 'results_and_value',
        'sections': [
            'Executive Summary (1 page max)',
            'Key Performance Indicators',
            'Campaign Highlights',
            'Month-over-Month Comparison',
            'Insights & Recommendations',
            'Next Month Focus'
        ],
        'design': {
            'branded': True,
            'logo_position': 'header',
            'color_scheme': 'client_brand',
            'include_disclaimers': True
        },
        'data_filters': {
            'show_raw_numbers': False,
            'show_confidence_intervals': False,
            'include_methodology': 'brief'
        }
    }
    
    INTERNAL_REPORT = {
        'audience': 'internal_team',
        'tone': 'analytical',
        'technical_level': 'high',
        'focus': 'detailed_analysis',
        'sections': [
            'Situation Summary',
            'Detailed Metrics',
            'Segment Analysis',
            'Statistical Analysis',
            'A/B Test Results',
            'Technical Issues',
            'Detailed Recommendations',
            'Appendix: Raw Data'
        ],
        'design': {
            'branded': False,
            'logo_position': 'none',
            'color_scheme': 'functional',
            'include_disclaimers': False
        },
        'data_filters': {
            'show_raw_numbers': True,
            'show_confidence_intervals': True,
            'include_methodology': 'full'
        }
    }
    
    EXECUTIVE_REPORT = {
        'audience': 'c_suite',
        'tone': 'strategic',
        'technical_level': 'minimal',
        'focus': 'business_impact',
        'sections': [
            'One-Page Summary',
            'Business Impact',
            'Strategic Opportunities',
            'Resource Requirements',
            'Decision Points'
        ],
        'design': {
            'branded': True,
            'logo_position': 'header',
            'color_scheme': 'executive',
            'include_disclaimers': False
        },
        'data_filters': {
            'show_raw_numbers': False,
            'show_confidence_intervals': False,
            'include_methodology': 'none'
        }
    }
    
    @classmethod
    def get_template(cls, audience):
        """Get template configuration for audience type"""
        templates = {
            'client': cls.CLIENT_REPORT,
            'internal': cls.INTERNAL_REPORT,
            'executive': cls.EXECUTIVE_REPORT
        }
        return templates.get(audience, cls.INTERNAL_REPORT)
    
    @classmethod
    def format_metric(cls, value, metric_type, audience):
        """Format metric based on audience"""
        template = cls.get_template(audience)
        
        if metric_type == 'currency':
            if audience == 'executive':
                return f"${value/1000000:.1f}M" if value >= 1000000 else f"${value/1000:.0f}K"
            elif audience == 'client':
                return f"${value:,.0f}"
            else:
                return f"${value:,.2f}"
        
        elif metric_type == 'percentage':
            if audience in ['executive', 'client']:
                return f"{value:.1f}%"
            else:
                return f"{value:.2f}%"
        
        elif metric_type == 'number':
            if audience == 'executive':
                if value >= 1000000:
                    return f"{value/1000000:.1f}M"
                elif value >= 1000:
                    return f"{value/1000:.0f}K"
            return f"{value:,.0f}"
        
        return str(value)
    
    @classmethod
    def generate_section_content(cls, section_name, data, audience):
        """Generate content appropriate for audience"""
        template = cls.get_template(audience)
        
        if section_name == 'Executive Summary':
            if audience == 'executive':
                return cls._generate_one_pager(data)
            elif audience == 'client':
                return cls._generate_client_summary(data)
            else:
                return cls._generate_detailed_summary(data)
        
        elif section_name == 'Key Performance Indicators':
            return cls._format_kpis(data, template['data_filters'])
        
        elif section_name == 'Recommendations':
            if audience == 'executive':
                return cls._generate_strategic_recommendations(data)
            elif audience == 'client':
                return cls._generate_client_recommendations(data)
            else:
                return cls._generate_tactical_recommendations(data)
        
        return data
    
    @staticmethod
    def _generate_one_pager(data):
        """Generate one-page executive summary"""
        return {
            'the_situation': 'Brief context (2-3 sentences)',
            'key_findings': ['Finding 1', 'Finding 2', 'Finding 3'],
            'business_impact': 'Revenue/efficiency impact',
            'recommended_action': 'Primary recommendation',
            'investment_required': 'Resources needed',
            'expected_outcome': 'Projected results'
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def _generate_client_summary(data):
        """Generate client-friendly summary"""
        return {
            'performance_overview': 'Overall performance narrative',
            'what_worked': ['Successful initiative 1', 'Successful initiative 2'],
            'areas_for_improvement': ['Opportunity 1', 'Opportunity 2'],
            'next_steps': 'What we recommend doing next'
        }
    
    @staticmethod
    def _generate_detailed_summary(data):
        """Generate detailed analytical summary"""
        return {
            'methodology': 'Detailed methodology description',
            'data_sources': ['Source 1', 'Source 2', 'Source 3'],
            'key_metrics': data.get('metrics'),
            'statistical_significance': 'p-values and confidence intervals',
            'limitations': 'Known data limitations'
        }
```
